originalCode:
# Catalog

**98 skills · 38 agents** — searchable reference for installed capabilities.

## Quick Lookup

**Need:** Feature implementation → `brainstorming` → `writing-plans` → `test-driven-development` → `subagent-driven-development`
**Need:** Bug/failure → `systematic-debugging` → `verification-before-completion`
**Need:** Code review → `requesting-code-review` (superpowers) or language-specific reviewer agent
**Need:** Research → `deep-research` (delegates to sub-agents) or `exa-search` (neural search)
**Need:** Design/UI → `ui-ux-pro-max` (67 styles, 161 palettes, 99 UX patterns)
**Need:** Security check → `security-review` skill or `security-reviewer` agent
**Need:** Build broken → language-specific build-resolver agent
**Need:** Documentation → `docs-lookup` (Context7) or `codebase-onboarding`

**Priority:** Superpowers > ECC when both match (`rules/superpowers-priority.md`)

---

## Superpowers (14 skills, 1 agent)

**Primary framework** — language-agnostic, triggers via mandatory activation checklist (`rules/superpowers-priority.md`).

| Skill | Trigger | Action |
|-------|---------|--------|
| **Workflow** |||
| `brainstorming` | Before creative/feature work | Explore intent, requirements, design |
| `writing-plans` | Multi-step task/complex feature | Step-by-step plan before code |
| `executing-plans` | Executing plan in separate session | Execute with review checkpoints |
| `subagent-driven-development` | Executing plan with independent tasks | Dispatch parallel sub-agents |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | 2+ independent tasks | Run simultaneously |
| **Quality** |||
| `test-driven-development` | Writing feature/bugfix | Tests first, then implement |
| `systematic-debugging` | Bug/test failure/unexpected behavior | Hypothesis → verify → fix |
| `verification-before-completion` | About to claim "done"/"fixed"/"passing" | Run verification, show evidence |
| `requesting-code-review` | Completing work/before merge | Review vs plan/standards |
| `receiving-code-review` | Receiving code review feedback | Verify technically, no blind agreement |
| **Git** |||
| `using-git-worktrees` | Starting feature needing isolation | Create isolated worktree + safety |
| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Implementation complete, tests pass | Options: merge, PR, cleanup |
| **Meta** |||
| `writing-skills` | Creating/editing skills | Skill authoring guidelines |
| `using-superpowers` | Starting any conversation | How to find/use skills |

**Agent:** `code-reviewer` — primary review agent (overrides ECC `code-reviewer`)

---

## ECC Skills (68 total)

**Agent & Automation (20)**

| Skill | Use When |
|-------|----------|
| `ck` | Auto: session start/end (Rules 1-2) — per-project memory |
| `continuous-learning` | Auto: session end (Rule 2) — extract reusable patterns |
| `continuous-learning-v2` | Instinct-based learning, confidence scoring |
| `strategic-compact` | Auto: ~20 tool calls (Rule 3) — context compaction |
| `autonomous-agent-harness` | Persistent memory, scheduled ops, task queues |
| `autonomous-loops` | Sequential pipelines → DAG systems |
| `continuous-agent-loop` | Quality gates, evals, recovery controls |
| `gan-style-harness` | Generator-Evaluator adversarial pattern |
| `agent-harness-construction` | Optimize action spaces, tool defs, observations |
| `agent-eval` | Head-to-head agent comparison (pass rate, cost, time) |
| `agent-payment-x402` | Per-task budgets, spending controls |
| `agentic-engineering` | Eval-first execution, 15-min units, model routing |
| `ai-first-engineering` | AI-heavy team operating model |
| `claude-devfleet` | Multi-agent orchestration in parallel worktrees |
| `dmux-workflows` | Multi-agent coordination via tmux panes |
| `team-builder` | Interactive agent picker, dispatch parallel teams |
| `ralphinho-rfc-pipeline` | RFC-driven DAG with merge queues, quality gates |
| `santa-method` | Two independent reviewers must both pass |
| `iterative-retrieval` | Progressive context refinement (subagent context problem) |
| `context-budget` | Audit Augment context, identify bloat |

**Architecture & Patterns (9)**

| Skill | Use When |
|-------|----------|
| `api-design` | Designing REST APIs — naming, codes, pagination, versioning |
| `backend-patterns` | Node/Express/Next.js server-side architecture |

| `hexagonal-architecture` | Ports & Adapters design (TS, Java, Kotlin, Go) |

|-------|----------|
| `tdd-workflow` | 80%+ coverage — unit, integration, E2E |
| `e2e-testing` | Playwright E2E — POM, CI/CD integration |
| `ai-regression-testing` | Sandbox API testing, catch AI blind spots |

## UI/UX Pro Max (1 skill)

Searchable design database: **67 styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, 25 chart types** across 16 tech stacks.

The agent auto-queries this when you ask about design, styling, or UI decisions. You can also search directly:

```bash
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech" --domain style -n 5
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "dashboard" --domain color -n 3
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "mobile" --domain ux -n 5
```

---

## Karpathy Guidelines (1)

From [Andrej Karpathy](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) — reduce LLM coding mistakes.

**4 principles:** Think Before Coding (wrong assumptions) · Simplicity First (overcomplication) · Surgical Changes (orthogonal edits) · Goal-Driven (tests-first, verifiable criteria)

**Working:** Minimal diffs, simple first-pass, questions before code, clean PRs.

Source: [multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills](https://github.com/multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills)

---

## ECC Agents (37)

Dispatch for specialized tasks.

### Language Reviewers (9 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `typescript-reviewer` | Type safety, async correctness, Node/web security |
| `python-reviewer` | PEP 8, Pythonic idioms, type hints, security |
| `go-reviewer` | Idiomatic Go, concurrency patterns, error handling |
| `rust-reviewer` | Ownership, lifetimes, error handling, unsafe usage |
| `java-reviewer` | Layered architecture, JPA patterns, security, concurrency |
| `kotlin-reviewer` | Coroutine safety, Compose, clean architecture |
| `cpp-reviewer` | Memory safety, modern C++ idioms, concurrency |
| `csharp-reviewer` | .NET conventions, async patterns, nullable types |
| `flutter-reviewer` | Widget best practices, state management, Dart idioms |

### Build Resolvers (8 agents)

| Agent | Fixes |
|-------|-------|
| `build-error-resolver` | TypeScript/JavaScript build and type errors |
| `cpp-build-resolver` | CMake, compilation, linker issues |
| `dart-build-resolver` | Flutter compilation, pub dependency conflicts |
| `go-build-resolver` | Go build, vet, linter warnings |
| `java-build-resolver` | Maven/Gradle, Java compiler errors |
| `kotlin-build-resolver` | Gradle, Kotlin compiler errors |
| `rust-build-resolver` | Cargo build, borrow checker issues |
| `pytorch-build-resolver` | Tensor shape mismatches, CUDA, gradient issues |

### Domain Specialists (8 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `architect` | System design, scalability, technical decisions |
| `planner` | Detailed implementation plans |
| `database-reviewer` | PostgreSQL query optimization, schema design |
| `security-reviewer` | OWASP Top 10, secrets, SSRF, injection |
| `performance-optimizer` | Bottlenecks, bundle size, memory leaks |
| `healthcare-reviewer` | Clinical safety, CDSS accuracy, PHI compliance |
| `doc-updater` | Documentation and codemap updates |
| `docs-lookup` | Up-to-date library docs via Context7 MCP |

### Testing & Quality (2 agents)

| Agent | Specializes in |
|-------|---------------|
| `tdd-guide` | Test-driven development methodology, 80%+ coverage |
| `e2e-runner` | E2E testing with Playwright, test journeys, flaky tests |

### GAN Harness (3 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `gan-planner` | Expands prompt into full product spec with sprints |
| `gan-generator` | Implements features, reads evaluator feedback, iterates |
| `gan-evaluator` | Tests live app via Playwright, scores against rubric |

### Open Source Pipeline (3 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `opensource-forker` | Fork project, strip secrets, replace internal refs |
| `opensource-sanitizer` | Scan for leaked secrets, PII, internal references |
| `opensource-packager` | Generate README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, setup.sh |

### Operations (4 agents)

| Agent | Role |
|-------|------|
| `chief-of-staff` | Triage email, Slack, LINE, Messenger communications |
| `harness-optimizer` | Optimize agent harness for reliability, cost, throughput |
| `loop-operator` | Monitor autonomous loops, intervene when stalled |
| `refactor-cleaner` | Dead code cleanup, unused imports, consolidation |

---

## Storage

| What | Where | Why |
|------|-------|-----|
| Skills | `skills/{superpowers,ecc,ui-ux-pro-max,learned}/` | Auto-discovered |
| Agents | `agents/{superpowers,ecc}/` | Auto-discovered |
| Rules | `rules/` | Always loaded |
| ck memory | `ck/` | No pollution, survives deletion |
| Learned | `skills/learned/` | Cross-project |
| Plugins | `plugins/marketplaces/` | Git updates |

---

## Session Lifecycle

```
Start → auto-resume/register
  ↓
Work → skills match
  ↓
Git → auto-save
  ↓
"bye" → save + continuous-learning
  ↓
Next → briefing
```

See `rules/session-lifecycle.md`.

## UI/UX Pro Max (1 skill)

Searchable design database: **67 styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, 25 chart types** across 16 tech stacks.

The agent auto-queries this when you ask about design, styling, or UI decisions. You can also search directly:

```bash
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "fintech" --domain style -n 5
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "dashboard" --domain color -n 3
python3 ~/.augment/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "mobile" --domain ux -n 5
```

---

## Karpathy Guidelines (1)

From [Andrej Karpathy](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) — reduce LLM coding mistakes.

**4 principles:** Think Before Coding (wrong assumptions) · Simplicity First (overcomplication) · Surgical Changes (orthogonal edits) · Goal-Driven (tests-first, verifiable criteria)

**Working:** Minimal diffs, simple first-pass, questions before code, clean PRs.

Source: [multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills](https://github.com/multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills)

## Storage

| What | Where | Why |
|------|-------|-----|
| Skills | `skills/{superpowers,ecc,ui-ux-pro-max,learned}/` | Auto-discovered |
| Agents | `agents/{superpowers,ecc}/` | Auto-discovered |
| Rules | `rules/` | Always loaded |
| ck memory | `ck/` | No pollution, survives deletion |
| Learned | `skills/learned/` | Cross-project |
| Plugins | `plugins/marketplaces/` | Git updates |

---

## Session Lifecycle

```
Start → auto-resume/register
  ↓
Work → skills match
  ↓
Git → auto-save
  ↓
"bye" → save + continuous-learning
  ↓
Next → briefing
```

See `rules/session-lifecycle.md`.
